title: Viewport selection + hover + interaction
analysis:
Task 95 adds interactive 3D selection to the viewport. The existing codebase follows a clean factory-function pattern: scene.ts, controls.ts, meshManager.ts each export a `create*` function returning a context object with methods and a dispose. Tests mock three.js via vi.mock('three') with inline mock classes.

The core new module is `selection.ts` — a `createSelection` factory that wraps Three.js Raycaster for pointer-based hover/click detection on meshes managed by meshManager. It will: (1) resolve pointer coords to entity paths via raycasting, (2) apply emissive highlight on hover, (3) create WireframeGeometry+LineSegments overlay on selection, (4) provide fitToView to frame all content, and (5) expose dispose for cleanup. Viewport.tsx will be extended to wire selection into the render loop, render a floating tooltip on hover, an evaluating spinner overlay when evalStatus.phase !== idle, and a fit-to-view button.

Key considerations: The meshManager already stores entity_path as mesh.name and exposes getSceneMeshes() returning Map<string, Mesh> — perfect for raycasting. The selectionStore has selectEntity/hoverEntity. The engineStore has evalStatus with phase 'idle'|'evaluating'|'resolving'. All Three.js dependencies will be mocked in tests following the established pattern.

The Viewport component currently accepts only {meshes} prop. It will be extended to accept optional callbacks (onHover, onSelect), evalStatus, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity, and a fitToView ref. This keeps the component testable with simple prop injection, matching the existing pattern from task 93 where stores are created in App and passed as props.

steps.description: Write failing tests for createSelection factory skeleton: returns object with setHovered, setSelected, fitToView, dispose methods. Mock Three.js Raycaster, WireframeGeometry, LineSegments, LineBasicMaterial, Box3, Vector3, Color. Follow same vi.mock('three') pattern as meshManager.test.ts.

Implement createSelection factory skeleton in selection.ts. Accepts {scene, camera, domElement, getMeshes: () => Map<string, Mesh>, onHover: (path: string|null) => void, onSelect: (path: string|null) => void}. Returns SelectionContext with stub methods (setHovered, setSelected, fitToView, dispose). Export from viewport/index.ts.

Write failing tests for pointer-based hover raycasting: simulate pointermove event on domElement, verify Raycaster.setFromCamera called with normalized device coordinates computed from event clientX/Y relative to domElement bounds, and raycaster.intersectObjects called with mesh array from getMeshes(). When intersection found, onHover called with mesh.name. When no intersection, onHover called with null.

Implement pointermove handler in createSelection: compute NDC from event coords and domElement rect, call raycaster.setFromCamera + intersectObjects on Array.from(getMeshes().values()), call onHover with first intersection's object.name or null. Register handler via domElement.addEventListener('pointermove', ...).

Write failing tests for hover emissive highlight: setHovered('A') applies emissive color (theme accent) to mesh A's MeshStandardMaterial. setHovered(null) resets emissive to black (0x000000). Changing hover from A to B resets A's emissive and highlights B's emissive. setHovered with unknown entity path is a no-op.

Implement setHovered in createSelection: track previousHoveredPath, look up mesh in getMeshes(), reset previous mesh's material.emissive to Color(0x000000), apply highlight Color to new mesh's material.emissive. Store highlight color as constant derived from THEME_TOKENS.accent.

Write failing tests for click-based selection raycasting: simulate pointerdown event on domElement, verify raycaster used same as hover, onSelect called with mesh.name on intersection, onSelect(null) on miss.
Implement pointerdown handler in createSelection: same raycast logic as hover, call onSelect. Register via domElement.addEventListener('pointerdown', ...).

Write failing tests for wireframe overlay on selection: setSelected('A') creates WireframeGeometry from mesh A's geometry, creates LineSegments with LineBasicMaterial(accent color), adds LineSegments to scene. setSelected(null) removes LineSegments from scene and disposes WireframeGeometry. Changing selection from A to B removes A's wireframe, creates B's. setSelected with unknown entity is a no-op (no wireframe, no crash).

Implement setSelected in createSelection: track current wireframe LineSegments object. On deselect, remove from scene and dispose geometry. On select, create WireframeGeometry from mesh.geometry, create LineSegments with LineBasicMaterial({color: accent}), add to scene.

Write failing tests for fitToView: with meshes in scene, fitToView computes bounding box via Box3.expandByObject for each mesh, calculates center and size, positions camera at appropriate distance based on bounding sphere radius and camera FOV, calls camera.lookAt(center). With no meshes, fitToView is a no-op (no crash).

Implement fitToView in createSelection: create Box3, iterate getMeshes() calling box.expandByObject(mesh), compute center via box.getCenter, compute size via box.getSize, calculate distance = max(sizeX, sizeY, sizeZ) / (2 * tan(camera.fov/2 * PI/180)), set camera.position to center offset by distance on Z, call camera.lookAt(center), camera.updateProjectionMatrix().
Write failing tests for dispose: verify domElement.removeEventListener called for both 'pointermove' and 'pointerdown' handlers. If wireframe exists, it is removed from scene and disposed.
Implement dispose in createSelection: remove pointermove and pointerdown listeners from domElement, clean up wireframe if present (remove from scene, dispose geometry).

Write failing tests for updated Viewport.tsx: (1) Viewport with onHover/onSelect props wires them through createSelection, (2) tooltip div with data-testid='viewport-tooltip' appears when hoveredEntity prop is set and shows the entity name, (3) tooltip hidden when hoveredEntity is null, (4) spinner overlay with data-testid='viewport-spinner' appears when evalStatus.phase is 'evaluating', (5) spinner hidden when evalStatus.phase is 'idle', (6) fit-to-view button with data-testid='fit-to-view' exists.

Update Viewport.tsx: extend ViewportProps with optional onHover, onSelect, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity, evalStatus, onFitToView. In onMount, create selection via createSelection, wire onHover/onSelect. Add createEffect watching hoveredEntity → call selection.setHovered, createEffect watching selectedEntity → call selection.setSelected. Render tooltip overlay (Show when hoveredEntity), spinner overlay (Show when evalStatus?.phase !== 'idle'), fit-to-view button. Dispose selection in onCleanup. Update viewport/index.ts exports for SelectionContext.

design_decisions.decision: Create selection.ts as a separate factory module following the same create* pattern as scene.ts, controls.ts, meshManager.ts
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with the established viewport module architecture. Each concern is isolated in its own factory, making testing straightforward with vi.mock.

design_decisions.decision: Selection module takes callback functions (onHover, onSelect) rather than directly importing selectionStore
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the selection module decoupled from SolidJS stores. The Viewport component wires callbacks to store methods, making the selection module purely Three.js-focused and easier to test without SolidJS reactivity.

design_decisions.decision: Separate setHovered/setSelected from pointer event handlers
design_decisions.rationale: Allows the store to drive visual state (highlight/wireframe) independently of pointer events. When selection changes programmatically (e.g., from property editor click), the wireframe and highlight can still be updated via props flowing through Viewport. Also enables easier testing of visual effects vs event handling.

design_decisions.decision: Use pointer events (pointermove, pointerdown) instead of mouse events
design_decisions.rationale: Pointer events are the modern standard, work with touch and stylus, and are used by OrbitControls internally. Consistent event model avoids conflicts.

design_decisions.decision: Wireframe uses LineBasicMaterial with theme accent color
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the Catppuccin Mocha theme already in use (THEME_TOKENS.accent = '#89b4fa'). WireframeGeometry+LineSegments is the standard Three.js approach for wireframe overlays that don't affect the original mesh material.

design_decisions.decision: fitToView is synchronous camera repositioning (not animated) in the initial implementation
design_decisions.rationale: Animation (tweening) requires either a dependency like tween.js or manual interpolation in the render loop. Starting with instant repositioning keeps complexity low; animation can be added later as an enhancement.

design_decisions.decision: Tooltip and spinner are absolutely-positioned divs inside the viewport container
design_decisions.rationale: CSS absolute positioning over the canvas is the standard pattern for 3D viewport overlays. No extra dependency needed. Styled with existing theme CSS variables.

design_decisions.decision: evalStatus, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity passed as props to Viewport
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the component testable with simple prop injection. The parent (App.tsx, from task 93) already has both stores and can thread them through. Avoids premature context coupling. Matches existing pattern where meshes are a prop.

reuse.how: Follow same pattern for createSelection: take dependencies as arguments, return context object with methods + dispose.

reuse.what: Three.js vi.mock pattern with inline mock classes
reuse.how: Mock Raycaster, WireframeGeometry, LineSegments, Box3, Vector3, LineBasicMaterial, Color using same vi.mock('three', () => { ... }) pattern.
Selection module uses this map to get raycastable meshes and look up meshes by entity_path for highlight/wireframe. Passed as getMeshes callback.
Viewport wires selection onHover/onSelect callbacks to these store methods, bridging Three.js events to SolidJS reactive state.
Use THEME_TOKENS.accent for wireframe color and hover highlight emissive. Use --reify-* CSS variables for tooltip/spinner styling.
Raycaster intersection returns the mesh whose .name is the entity_path, providing the key for store updates and getMeshes() lookups.
Viewport checks evalStatus.phase !== 'idle' to show/hide the spinner overlay.

reuse.what: Viewport.test.tsx mock patterns (ResizeObserver stub, RAF capture, module-level vi.mock for scene/controls/meshManager)
reuse.how: Extend existing mock setup to include selection module mock. Add tests for new overlay elements.
